title: Bitdeer Q1 2026 Earnings: Key Takeaways
text: Bitdeer reported Q1 2026 revenue of $188.9 million, a 170% year-over-year increase, though down sequentially from $224.8 million in Q4 2025 due to lower Bitcoin prices and internal SEALMINER allocation. Adjusted EBITDA reached $14.4 million, up approximately $60 million annually but declining from the prior quarter's $24.3 million. The company posted a negative gross margin of -20.7% (-$39 million), driven by three factors: depressed Bitcoin prices, $70 million in noncash mining rig depreciation, and seasonal power costs. Self-mining hash rate surged to 65 exahashes per second by March 2026, representing over 400% year-over-year growth. Bitcoin production reached 668-705 units monthly, with April rebounding to 783 Bitcoin. The newly launched SEALMINER A4 series achieved industry-leading efficiency at 9.45 joules per terahash for the flagship Ultrahydro model, improving the overall fleet efficiency to 16.4 joules per terahash. Annual recurring revenue for AI services exploded from $10 million in January to $69 million by April 2026. GPU utilization climbed from 41% to 94%, with over 4,000 GPUs deployed by April. Management noted a 40% increase in H100 hourly pricing without customer friction, with most contracts spanning 3-5 years. Tydal, Norway remains the flagship colocation opportunity, a 180-megawatt facility expected to become Norway's largest operational AI data center by December 2026. Negotiations are in advanced stages. Cash position improved to $297.7 million following a $375 million convertible notes offering. Total borrowings reached approximately $1.92 billion. Operating cash burn improved 42% sequentially to -$346.9 million.
